title: End of Evangelion A Multitude of Observations
display_title: End of Evangelion A Multitude of Observations
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion - A Multitude of Observations             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Sun Oct 31, 2010 12:11 am I remember reading a thread all about the Biblical, Judeo-Christian and Kabbalistic references Neon Genesis has to offer, and I decided to start a thread topic concerning somthing like the above, but a little different. Not exactly that numeral thread about, well... Numbers, but about a few thing people really don't notice about EoE. For starters, we all remember the scene where Giant Naked Rei/Lilith absorbs the Tree of Life into her/its forehead, right? Well, I noticed something pretty, well... 18+ right there. Clitoris and all, we see the thing open up a little, take in the Lance, and dissapear. Any reason for this has yet to be explained. Also, concerning the live action scene, the clips are taken from Anno-sama's hometown, if I'm not mistaken. Any insight as for the reason of these first two particular sightings, or if you noticed something else in Eoe (please don't bring up the final scene when Teh Rei does the "Now you see me, now you don't" trick. We all know, it's from the first episode.), please don't hesitate to bring it up![/spoiler]

[]: Re: End of Evangelion - A Multitude of Observations             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 31, 2010 2:31 am Tabby-chan wrote: the scene where Giant Naked Rei/Lilith absorbs the Tree of Life into her/its forehead, right?

[]: Re: End of Evangelion - A Multitude of Observations             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Oct 31, 2010 1:10 pm Tabby-chan wrote: Clitoris and all, we see the thing open up a little, take in the Lance, and dissapear. Any reason for this has yet to be explained.

[]:             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Sun Oct 31, 2010 2:10 pm Well, I can see why you would think that. Anno created the character "Rei", and so he would have every right to. God knows how many anime characters I've drawn and screwed on the spot. Back to the topic, I was just reading the "Every Number Except 303" thread, and I noticed that someone stated, quote, "This would also explain why 13 is the unlucky number. 666 is the number of perfection, so if you use 667, perfection loops back to chaos." In Rebuild, as well as the manga, there are only 12 Angels (in Rebuild, this is unconfirmed). 12 would be the number of completion, so when one adds the number 13 (symbolising the Lilim here), completion is broken. For those of you who don't agree with me on the topic, "666 is the number of perfection", read the Book of Revalations. It says, "Wisdom is needed here. Let the one with wisdom solve the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man (or, "of mankind"). His number is 666". When God created man, the Lilim was innocent, perfect. Satan is often seen as a thief (and all other ungodly things), so he "stole" the number of man, 666, if Eve (Eva) (Lilith) ate from the Tree of Knowledge. Man's number likely went up one, to 667. Now, man is not perfect. The same kind of "stealing" happens in modern culture today. For example, the "Nazi sign", AKA Swastica, is actually a Christian symbol. Hitler also claimed to be a Christian. In this case, however, this does not make sense. He was trying to kill innocent Jews, when Jesus was a Jew as well (Jesus did not create Christianity. Man is responsible for that one). Well, Christianity, like Islam (the angels from Eva are taken from the Book of Enoch), stems from Judaism. So if Hitler's goal was to kill Jews, and he was indeed a Christian (which he wasn't), he would have to kill himself as well. The Ku Klux Klan also uses Christian scripture as an excuse or racism. Again, this makes no sense, because Christianity specifically rejects racism. Anyways, I would still like to see someone tell me more about the Biblical references in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Oct 31, 2010 8:37 pm Tabby-chan wrote: Back to the topic, I was just reading the "Every Number Except 303" thread, and I noticed that someone stated, quote, "This would also explain why 13 is the unlucky number. 666 is the number of perfection, so if you use 667, perfection loops back to chaos." In Rebuild, as well as the manga, there are only 12 Angels (in Rebuild, this is unconfirmed). 12 would be the number of completion, so when one adds the number 13 (symbolising the Lilim here), completion is broken. For those of you who don't agree with me on the topic, "666 is the number of perfection", read the Book of Revalations. It says, "Wisdom is needed here. Let the one with wisdom solve the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man (or, "of mankind"). His number is 666". When God created man, the Lilim was innocent, perfect. Satan is often seen as a thief (and all other ungodly things), so he "stole" the number of man, 666, if Eve (Eva) (Lilith) ate from the Tree of Knowledge. Man's number likely went up one, to 667. Now, man is not perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Tue Nov 02, 2010 6:05 pm Concerning the swastica, it is yet ANOTHER form of the cross. I don't quite get it either, but hey, what's true is true, I guess... Anyways, I don't recall the Bible being against Judaism. Besides, all Christians are, by default, Jews. I also don't recall any form of particular racism in the Biblical texts. If you meant Babylon, Rome, or such nations, it was because their people were wicked and evil and corrupt. The authors did not hate people from those particular countries. To further prove my point, have you read the story of Sodom and Gormorahh? Lot, if we recall, was told that the city would be reduced to ashes. However, as much as he despised this evil city, his brother (or someone he was close to, I don't rememmber exactly who) also resided in that city. He even went out of his way to pray that the city was not destroyed at all to save his brother. This did not work, obviously, given the circumstances, but he was not being racist to that nation. I think he was even born there. (I THINK, I don't know for certain.) Anyways, I did notice (now, I'm off-topic) that, judging from the dialouge shown in the last two episodes, Misato could be considered this "Great Prostitute". Not to say that Misato is a bad character, but the line "So, you'll sleep with anyone, Major?" hints that Misato would play this role. In End of Evangelion, this also happens in a small way. Misato says, "an adult kiss, let's do the rest when you get back." ,hinting that Misato would reward Shinji's return with... Yea. Also, the same happens with Hyuga, when he see's Misato's "ghost". Also, both 666 and 616 are used. This has been debated throughout history, but the standard Bible reads 666. This makes even more sense in Evangelion, EoE in particular, the "666 Firewall" being the Biblical reference here.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 02, 2010 6:16 pm Tabby-chan wrote: Concerning the swastica, it is yet ANOTHER form of the cross.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Tue Nov 02, 2010 6:39 pm Tabby-chan wrote: Concerning the swastica, it is yet ANOTHER form of the cross. I don't quite get it either, but hey, what's true is true, I guess...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 02, 2010 7:16 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: If you're trying to compare to Swastika to the cross the only way that can be done is that before the used the Swastika it was a religous symbol for Hinduism and Buddhism. So in those religons the Swastika would be their equivalent to a cross, but when used in the Nazi sense it loses that meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Nov 02, 2010 8:44 pm 616 is what's in the oldest known manuscripts, and the “standard Bible” is a piece of shit. There is no English translation of the Bible.

[]: Re: End of Evangelion - A Multitude of Observations             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Dec 21, 2010 10:02 am Tabby-chan wrote: Any reason for this has yet to be explained.

Not exactly that numeral thread about, well... Numbers, but about a few thing people really don't notic
